name: Sucarita Misra
dates_notes: Sucarita Miśra is conventionally placed in the eleventh century by his position in the Bhāṭṭa Mīmāṃsā sub-commentarial tradition on Kumārila.
core_thesis: Sucarita Miśra is a Bhāṭṭa Mīmāṃsaka and one of the principal sub-commentators on Kumārila's *Śloka-Vārttika*. His *Kāśikā* is not an independent system replacing Kumārila; it is a detailed exegetical aid to the Bhāṭṭa positions on Vedic authority, intrinsic validity, perception, word-meaning, and the critique of rival Buddhist and Brahmanical views as they appear in the *Śloka-Vārttika*. The timeline already treats Kumārila as the central author of mature Bhāṭṭa epistemology; Sucarita's entry records the commentarial layer through which that system was studied and stabilized. The source is already on disk at `data/sources/sanskrit/mimamsa/sucaritamisra_sloka_varttika.txt`.

engaged_works.title: Kasika on Kumarila's Shloka-Varttika
engaged_works.composition_dates_notes: Eleventh-century placement follows Sucarita's conventional position in the post-Kumārila Bhāṭṭa tradition.
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely attributed to Sucarita Miśra as a principal commentary on Kumārila's Śloka-Vārttika.

ascription_evidence.description: Bhāṭṭa Mīmāṃsā tradition treats Sucarita's Kāśikā as a principal sub-commentary on the Śloka-Vārttika.

engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit sub-commentary on Kumārila Bhaṭṭa's *Śloka-Vārttika*. The work explains and defends the Bhāṭṭa reading of the *Tarka-Pāda* topics: the definition of dharma, Vedic authorlessness, intrinsic validity, perception, word-meaning, and the critique of rival accounts. Its importance is exegetical and doctrinally conservative: it preserves the scholastic detail needed to understand how Kumārila's compact verses became a working Mīmāṃsā curriculum.
